title: ConvertKit vs ActiveCampaign (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: ConvertKit vs ActiveCampaign: compare pricing, features, and real use cases to find the right email tool for your needs.
intro: Choosing between ConvertKit and ActiveCampaign comes down to your specific use case. Both are legitimate email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences. ConvertKit is built for creators, bloggers, and course sellers, while ActiveCampaign targets businesses needing advanced automation and CRM. This comparison covers the key differences so you can decide quickly.

comparison_table.tool_b: Businesses Needing Advanced Automation And Crm

choose_a_if: You are primarily focused on creators, bloggers, and course sellers
choose_b_if: You are primarily focused on businesses needing advanced automation and CRM

faq.a: It depends on your use case. ConvertKit wins for creators, bloggers, and course sellers, while ActiveCampaign is stronger for businesses needing advanced automation and CRM. Neither is objectively better — the right choice depends on your specific needs.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, ConvertKit or ActiveCampaign?
faq.a: ActiveCampaign starts at a lower price point ($15/mo vs $29/mo). However, pricing scales with subscriber count, so the total cost depends on your list size.

faq.q: Can I switch from ActiveCampaign to ConvertKit?
faq.a: Yes, switching from ActiveCampaign to ConvertKit is possible. Most platforms support CSV import/export for subscriber lists. Plan for some time to recreate automations and templates, as these rarely transfer directly between platforms.
